chunks.text: Carlo H. Séquin is Professor Emeritus at UC Berkeley's EECS department. His contact information includes office at 639 Soda Hall, phone 510-642-5103, fax 510-643-1289, and email sequin@cs.berkeley.edu. Office hours are available on his homepage. He has a personal homepage, publications list, dissertation information, and research support details. Administrative assistant Leslie Goldstein can be reached at 765 Soda Hall, phone 3-2469, email lgolds@berkeley.edu.

Carlo Séquin earned his Ph.D. in experimental physics from University of Basel, Switzerland in 1969. He worked at Bell Telephone Laboratories from 1970-1976 on Charge-Coupled Devices and was introduced to computer graphics by Ken Knowlton. He joined UC Berkeley's EECS faculty in 1977, teaching VLSI courses to bridge CS and EE divisions. In the early 1980s, he co-introduced the RISC concept with David Patterson. He served as head of the Computer Science Division from 1980-1983. He is a Fellow of ACM and IEEE, and member of Swiss Academy of Engineering Sciences.

Séquin's research areas include Graphics (GR), Human-Computer Interaction (HCI), geometric modeling, artistic geometry, mathematical visualizations, and CAD tools. He is affiliated with several research centers: Berkeley Center for New Media (BCNM), Berkeley Institute of Design (BID), Center for Information Technology Research (CITRIS), and Visual Computing Lab (VCL).

Selected publications include work on interactive visualization of architectural models (1995), functional optimization for surface design (1992), OPASYN compiler for CMOS operational amplifiers (1990), RISC architectures for VLSI computers (1986), and Charge Transfer Devices book (1975). Honors include Berkeley Citation (2016), CS Technical Achievement Award (2003), ACM Fellow (1997), and Diane S. McEntyre Teaching Award (1996).
